summary:
The observation of a consistent 0.5 mg elimination every 4 hours indicates a medication with predictable, linear pharmacokinetics amenable to periodic serologic monitoring. Among common anticoagulants, only apixaban meets these criteria—being orally available, exhibiting stable plasma levels, and possessing a reasonably uniform half-life allowing such systematic quantification through repeated blood draws.  

Final answer: $\boxed{\text{Apixaban}}$  
(Note: Although other agents have some predictability, only apixaban aligns fully with both administration route and observable elimination dynamics.) Based solely on this information, the most likely candidate is apixaban.
